claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 10 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 19 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 8 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

To begin, could you tell me: **Do you have responsibility for a child or children?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have responsibility for children. I have three kids - Alex who's 10, Blake who's 19, and Charlie who's 2.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have three children - Alex (10), Blake (19), and Charlie (2). Let me assess eligibility for each of them.

Let me start by asking some questions:

**First, regarding Blake who is 19:** Is Blake currently in approved education or training? (This would typically be full-time non-advanced education such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or approved training courses.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment. He finished school a while ago and he's just at home right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since Blake is 19 and not in approved education or training, he would not be eligible for Child Benefit as it only extends beyond age 16 (up to age 20) for young people in approved education or training.

Now let me ask about your other children:

**Do Alex (10) and Charlie (2) normally live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me, yes. But Charlie doesn't live with me - he lives with his mum.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

**Regarding Charlie who lives with his mum:** Do you contribute to Charlie's upkeep (for example, through maintenance payments or other financial support)? And if so, do you contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit weekly rate would be?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I don't really contribute anything financially at the moment - things have been tight and I haven't been able to help out with money for Charlie.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for being honest about that. 

Just to clarify on the living arrangements: **Does Charlie live with his mum full-time, or does he spend some time living with you as well (for example, staying with you on certain days of the week)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he lives with his mum full-time. I don't have him staying with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. 

Now, let me ask a few more questions about Alex who lives with you:

**Are you and Alex both living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to ask:

**Is Alex in full-time care (such as being looked after by the local authority) or has Alex been in hospital for more than 12 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex was in care for a bit recently - just for a couple of months - but he's back home with me now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. Let me clarify:

**Is Alex currently in care right now, or is he fully back living with you at home?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's fully back living with me at home now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just a couple more questions:

**Do you receive any benefits or financial support for Alex from your local authority (for example, payments because he was previously in care)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't receive any financial support from the local authority for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Are you the person with the main responsibility for Alex? In other words, is there anyone else (like another parent or guardian) who also has Alex living with them or shares the main day-to-day care for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm the person with the main responsibility for Alex. He lives with me and I take care of him day-to-day. No one else has him living with them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your Child Benefit eligibility for each of your three children.

Let me summarize what you've told me:

- **Alex (10)**: Lives with you in the UK, you have main responsibility for him, he's back living with you full-time (not currently in care), and you don't receive local authority payments for him.

- **Blake (19)**: Not in approved education or training.

- **Charlie (2)**: Lives full-time with his mum, and you don't contribute financially to his upkeep.

Based on the UK Child Benefit rules:

**You would be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.** He is under 16, lives with you, and you have main responsibility for him.

**You would NOT be eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.** Child Benefit stops when a young person turns 20, or when they turn 16 if they're not in approved education or training. Since Blake is 19 and not in education or training, he doesn't qualify.

**You would NOT be eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.** When a child doesn't live with you, you can only claim Child Benefit if you contribute to their upkeep at a rate at least equal to the Child Benefit amount. Since Charlie lives with his mum full-time and you're not currently contributing financially, his mum would be the one eligible to claim (as she has him living with her).

**In summary: You can claim Child Benefit for Alex only.**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Thank you so much for explaining all that. That's really helpful to know I can claim for Alex at least. I appreciate you taking the time to go through each of my children's situations with me.
